methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 25 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 42.9x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Southern Launch is a growth-stage space and aerospace company founded by Lloyd Damp. The valuation universe comprises 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private competitors including SpaceX, Rocket Lab, Blue Origin, Sierra Space, and Planet Labs, representing a total of 13 comparable entities in the Space & Aerospace sector.

Two standard triangulation methods were evaluated. Peer-median multiples could not be applied due to insufficient narrow comparables: while 25 same-sector peers exist in the broader Space & Aerospace universe, filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar valuation tier yielded fewer than three datapoints, making a subset median unreliable. Public-comp anchoring using the sector's P/S multiple proved inapplicable because Southern Launch's revenue is not on file—a common constraint with private company analysis. The Space & Aerospace public median trades at 42.9x sales across the five listed tickers, but without disclosed target revenue, this anchor cannot be operationalized.

No triangulated valuation range can be supported from the available data. Meaningful analysis would require either (1) disclosure of Southern Launch's revenue or revenue guidance, enabling P/S multiple application, or (2) identification of at least three directly comparable private funding rounds in early-to-mid stage space infrastructure or launch services. The primary uncertainty remains revenue scale; absent that input, sector median multiples remain contextual reference only rather than applicable valuation drivers.
